name: Talavera de la Reina, Alcaudete de la Jara, Calera y Chozas – circular on the Vía Verde de la Jara
name_original: Talavera de la Reina, Alcaudete de la Jara, Calera y Chozas – circular en la Vía Verde de la Jara
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Talavera De La Reina are situated on the fertile plains of the Tagus and Alberche rivers in the province of Toledo, Spain. The region features diverse landscapes, including riverside trails along the Tagus River and the dedicated La Jara Greenway. Cyclists can explore areas ranging from farmland to the distinct natural scenery of the Toledo steppe. For more challenging rides, the Sierra de San Vicente offers varied terrain. Best touring cycling routes around Talavera De La Reina The most popular touring cycling route is Talavera de la Reina to Oropesa – circular route through the Toledo steppe, a 47.9 miles (77.1 km) trail that takes about 5 hours 42 minutes to complete. This route traverses the Toledo steppe, offering distinct natural scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Talavera de la Reina on bike lane – circular route, an easy 19.0 miles (30.6 km) path. This route is ideal for all skill levels, featuring mostly paved surfaces and manageable elevation gains. Local touring cyclists also love the Talavera de la Reina, Alcaudete de la Jara, Calera y Chozas – circular on the Vía Verde de la Jara, a 44.0 miles (70.8 km) trail leading through the Vía Verde de la Jara, often completed in about 4 hours 11 minutes. Touring cycling around Talavera De La Reina is defined by the Tagus River, the La Jara Greenway, and the Toledo steppe. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy bike lane routes to more challenging trails. The routes in Talavera De La Reina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Talavera De La Reina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling around Talavera De La Reina?
A: The region offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll find pleasant riverside trails along the Tagus River, with local flora like holm oaks and poplars. Routes also traverse the distinct natural scenery of the Toledo steppe, and for more challenging rides, the varied terrain of the Sierra de San Vicente. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022697552/talavera-de-la-reina-alcaudete-de-la-jara-calera-y-chozas-circular-on-the-via-verde-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Jara&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you through farmland and forested areas.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Talavera de la Reina offers several easy routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030619709/talavera-de-la-reina-on-bike-lane-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera de la Reina on bike lane – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option, featuring mostly paved surfaces and manageable elevation gains, making it ideal for all skill levels and shorter rides.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022697231/talavera-de-la-reina-to-oropesa-circular-route-through-the-toledo-steppe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera de la Reina to Oropesa – circular route through the Toledo steppe&lt;/a&gt; require very good fitness, involving significant elevation changes and potentially unpaved sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022697955/talavera-de-la-reina-and-senda-de-viriato-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera de la Reina and Senda de Viriato Loop&lt;/a&gt; also presents diverse and challenging terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Talavera De La Reina?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions for cycling in this region of Spain, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. These seasons provide comfortable temperatures for longer rides and enjoying the natural scenery.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as circular loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022697231/talavera-de-la-reina-to-oropesa-circular-route-through-the-toledo-steppe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera de la Reina to Oropesa – circular route through the Toledo steppe&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030619709/talavera-de-la-reina-on-bike-lane-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera de la Reina on bike lane – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022697552/talavera-de-la-reina-alcaudete-de-la-jara-calera-y-chozas-circular-on-the-via-verde-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera de la Reina, Alcaudete de la Jara, Calera y Chozas – circular on the Vía Verde de la Jara&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I explore near the cycling routes?
A: Talavera de la Reina is rich in history and culture. You can visit the Basilica of Our Lady of Prado, known for its 16th-century tiles, or the Ruiz de Luna Pottery Museum. Other notable sites include the collegiate church of Santa María la Mayor, the Roman Bridge, and the ancient Wall and Albarrana Tower. Within the city, you can also find green spaces like Prado Garden and Alameda Park. You can find more details about these at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5013825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera de la Reina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Talavera De La Reina?
A: The touring cycling routes in Talavera De La Reina are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from riverside paths to the Vía Verde de la Jara, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any specific routes that follow the Vía Verde de la Jara?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022697552/talavera-de-la-reina-alcaudete-de-la-jara-calera-y-chozas-circular-on-the-via-verde-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera de la Reina, Alcaudete de la Jara, Calera y Chozas – circular on the Vía Verde de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; is a dedicated route that allows you to experience this greenway, traversing diverse landscapes from farmland to forested areas.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Talavera De La Reina?
A: There are over 90 touring cycling routes available around Talavera De La Reina, catering to various skill levels and preferences. This includes a good mix of easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: Are there any notable bridges or architectural features along the routes?
A: Yes, the region features several interesting bridges. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4966496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilla-La Mancha Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5190803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reina Sofía Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which are prominent landmarks in the area.

Q: Can I find places to eat or rest along the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific bike-friendly cafes or restaurants are not detailed in the route information, many routes pass through or near towns and villages where you can typically find amenities for refreshments and rest. It's always a good idea to plan your stops in advance, especially on longer routes.

Q: Is there public transport available for cyclists in Talavera De La Reina?
A: Information regarding specific public transport options for cyclists (e.g., trains or buses that accommodate bikes) is not readily available. It's advisable to check local transport provider websites for the most current policies and schedules if you plan to combine cycling with public transport.
tips:
It extends along the eastern bank of the Tagus River and is the second most populated municipality in the province and the fourth in Castilla-La Mancha.
Among its monuments are the Basilica of Our Lady of the Prado, which houses what is today the patron saint of the city. It was called Queen of the Hermitages by Philip II, it is also called the Sistine Chapel of ceramics, because this colorful art shows here all its splendor from the 16th century to the present day. The style of its construction is a combination of Renaissance and Baroque, although it has undergone various restorations over the centuries. It was elevated to the dignity of Basilica in 1989 by Pope John Paul II.
In the Church of Santa María La Mayor (La Colegial) you can admire its pure Gothic cloister, which was built around the year 1469. Its floor plan is rectangular, made up of three longitudinal naves and without a transept. In the years 1704 and 1705 the two upper sections of the Tower were added.

Alcaudete de la Jara is in a deep plain, surrounded by hills of medium elevation. It is known as "Levante de Castilla" thanks to its extraordinary microclimate, which makes it enjoy very mild temperatures throughout the year.

The term Calera is due to the stone quarries to make lime that existed in the area. The name Calera y Chozas was given when Calera joined the town of Las Chozas de Talavera. There is an abundant and varied fauna and vegetation in the area. The Greenway route starts on the same platform as the railway station.
